is_p_falciparum.evidence: Study of Plasmodium falciparum: 'at least 95% of infections being due to P. falciparum'; genotyping of pfcrt-76 and pfmdr1-86.

reports_target_markers.evidence: Reports pfcrt K76T (position 76) and pfmdr1 N86Y (position 86), both curated target codons.

reports_extractable_frequencies.evidence: 409/999 carried pfcrt-76T (40.9%); pfmdr1-86Y present 44.4% (91) ITC and 44.6% (91) non-ITC; frequencies by group given.

sub_country_location.evidence: Study performed in Ziniaré and Boussé health districts (sub-national), but frequencies are reported only aggregated across 18 villages split into ITC vs non-ITC groups, not tied to individual named sub-national locations. Data are grouped by intervention arm rather than by district/village geography.

temporal_resolution_within_3y.evidence: In vivo study of CQ efficacy conducted August to November 2002 — sampling within a single year.

african_field_samples.evidence: Field/clinical isolates from children with uncomplicated malaria in Burkina Faso, West Africa.

is_primary_study.evidence: Primary in vivo efficacy and molecular study with enrolled children and genotyping results.

duplicate_risk.evidence: 'Details of the study within which this work was nested have been published elsewhere [27]' — Diallo DA et al. Am J Trop Med Hyg 2007;76:237–44, describing the same 2002 CQ efficacy study and pfcrt/pfmdr1 distributions.

supplement.evidence: No supplementary tables referenced; data are in main text and tables.

assessment.summary: Primary in vivo chloroquine-efficacy study in Burkina Faso (Ziniaré and Boussé health districts, 2002) genotyping pfcrt-76T and pfmdr1-86Y in P. falciparum from children. Reports extractable marker frequencies (e.g., 40.9% pfcrt-76T; ~44% pfmdr1-86Y) but only stratified by ITC vs non-ITC intervention arms rather than tied to specific named sub-national locations. Marker and temporal criteria are met, but the sub-country spatial resolution is questionable, and there is a nested/prior-published companion dataset (ref 27) posing high duplicate risk.
exclusion_reasons: Frequencies are aggregated by intervention arm (ITC vs non-ITC) across 18 villages rather than tied to specific named sub-national units/villages
High duplicate risk: study is nested within and its resistance-marker distributions published in Diallo et al. AJTMH 2007 (ref 27)
